messages.text: Shawn Jewkes
Camara Fault maintenance is in the line inspecting
While inspecting it was noticed all molds are getting scratched against something.
Maintenance is trying to locate where it’s coming from

We inspected different sections of the cooler, the demolder belt, and the product itself. At this time, I am not seeing any FM-P on the product itself or on the demolder belt. It would be beneficial to identify the root cause or primary source of the issue, as there is a possibility the chip on the molds can worsen over time.
Tooth was skipped in demolding area. Maintenance is working on fixing the issue
Let’s get the area clean where the source is at please. QA will conduct a visual inspection afterwards
We have to create a space and then run it around

The main drive sprocket was off a tooth, I suspect it happened during almond clean. There was a chain issue over the weekend that was mitigated and we haven't run with molds in the machine continuously until recently so the main problem was not noticed. It's hard to see this without molds as a reference. It's corrected now and we are making a gap for quality inspection.
It’s passed by QA
We are back and running

Nathan,
Will you (or Imperial) put the Pre-Treat temperature back to 115.0 when you have a moment? The buffer tank temperature is nice and toasty so we don't need the temper unit to work as hard.
You got it!
No issues with the Metal Detector
Mold Heather issues maintenance in the line inspecting
One of the 4 mold heaters overheated and faulted out the line. It will let them reset when the mold heater gets down below a certain point for a certain time
I must correct that, it did not fault out the line. It just throws an alarm and turns off the heaters but does not stop any production.

Also, after trending the heaters it seems like we have a failing blower fan on one of the heaters again. I have increased the fault limit a bit and at 65% duty cycle it doesn't fault. It's still within safe limits for the equipment.
Bosch is not sealing. Maintenance is working on it
Tempers have been good since the drop to 115 Robert Villarreal .4s and .3s
Nice
Running again
2nd clean Cavana
Start-of-shift checks (3rd shift): part weight, organoleptic properties, packaging, water test, labeling, and temperature—all within the correct range.
Change of lot CC26164I1 and date 11/FEB/28
C6 belt is moving to the south side maintenance is working on it
Start running back up
C1 belt down maintenance has been called
Shawn Jewkes  WE KEEP HAVING ISSUES WITH  FW  NOT CUTTING
Maintenance saved the recipe in the cavanna
MD and C6 belts are running correctly
Screen damage free
Temper in the range
Missing 3 wrench's
Belts C3 and C6 are running good
Maintenance is going to make adjustments to the cavanna we have lowered to 5 mpm
Sollich 3a
Charity Young, do you have anymore information on the KKL Mac nuts that are on hold in SV?
I do not
I will see what I can find out.
Ok, as soon as you do can you reach out to me?
Yes I will.

Curt, I like your idea of moving the cart next to the line, so that it’s no longer in the way of the fire extinguisher. 
Long term, I agree that it would be nice to move the fire extinguisher, once we have guard railing in place.

Curt Willis Dakota Adair Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth  can someone help us find the employees that parked infront of our roll up door please and tell them to move.  We are unloading equipment to take it inside the building
I'm sure it's 2nd shift employees
One of those pickup trucks belongs to Delia QA.
She is going to move it now.
Cones got moved around, so I don't think they had much understanding that they couldn't park there. I'll tell the people that are helping me to always make sure that there's cones there
Dakota Adair please help Marco Rodriguez out on this and coach EE’s on parking in this area
These areas need to be fixed / taped. Jody Howse Brandon Hernandez
Curt Willis
Charity Young
Level 2 clean complete
End 3 shift
End of shift Shadow board

Jeffrey Barney The team was able to finish sorting through these pallets. The front pallet is good FG and the rear pallet is unwrapped chocolate. IMS was gone for the night so we could not label them. Could you take care of this Monday please?

Checking 2pm
51 pieces
End of 1 shift
The operator noticed something leaking at Chicane Belt #5. Maintenance inspected it and identified the fluid as glycol, stating that we need to stop the line.

An e-stop was pressed on S2 on tunnel 3. 

We’ve turned it off and will be doing a re-heat.
I also agree with stopping the line so we can investigate further. Elizabeth Hernandez Dakota Adair Charity Young Miriah Manzanares
Curt Willis
We can observe the edge of the band
Thank you for addressing the issue.  Post updates on here once you get things cleaned and resolved
Agreed

Evaluating this incident, and the amount I can see below the band, this must have been happening for several hours, although I didn't see the glycol in direct contact with the product what was observed was significant. Do you think we should put production on hold? Charity Young Miriah Manzanares
We've finished the reheat; we've already set the program.

Investigating together with the maintenance team, we confirmed that the type of glycol we use for cooling the belts is approved for use with food. 
This liquid did not come into contact with the product; it was only found under the Chicane #5 belt and in drops that fell to the floor. It also did not migrate to the following belts.
At this time, maintenance released the belt so that production could have access to clean it better. 
Once the cleaning and liquid removal are complete, we will conduct an inspection together with ATP and production will begin, which will be monitored. 
Maintenance is working to find the source of the leak. 
We will be providing updates on this incident.
I agree, thanks.
After discussing with Dakota and Chartity. No i do not believe it is necessary. Please review the last hours visual checks in safety chain and look for any oil residue on product.
Jon, Scott and I went through and tightened all the hose clamps on the glycol chilling plates. We will run with the chiller off for the final 6 hrs
The belt is already clean; Maintenance is going to install the parts they removed.
QA will perform the ATP tests.
EEs told me belt is not ready for ATP. I'll update the result once is done
The team is finished, and QA is testing now.
The chocolate is tempered, and caramel is being sent to the Conbar.
Belt ready to run
Good job getting back up and running
We are having problems with Cavanna 4, and this is the alarm that is appearing; maintenance is here and is going to reset the Cavanna.
The problem did not clear like it had earlier. I believe the upper jaw thermocouple is bad
We are going to stop the line because the product is piling up very quickly, since only one Cavanna machine is running.
We currently have 29 containers of accumulated product.
Maintenance continues working at Cavanna 4.
Maintenance has already replaced the temperature cable.
Cavanna 3 is working well now; we're going to run the line.
Caleb York thank you
I try
This error popped up on cavanna 3
I told Elizabeth about it earlier. It's not affecting the print, it's an error with the program. Whenever you need to do a lot code change, power cycle it
Ok thanks
Finished the line clearance and set up. Starting FD pouches

Team,

The drain underneath the buffer tank and sweco was found dirty. We will be performing a heavy wash and getting the area cleaned up. 

Starting Monday we will need to hit the filler side drains with the 800ppm sanitizer x3 per shift. Starting from underneath the buffer tank down to the curtain. 

Alex Mendoza Brandon Lance Maria Caldera Wilson Amaris Cabrera Hervin Herrera Alan Todd Miriah Manzanares
There is one sensor out of range and we cannot reset it. We have tried unplugging/plugging, powering down and reloading the recipe. This will need looked at Monday morning. Jeffrey Barney Dave Ruff

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.
8:30 we paused the line to run out 30 bins of accumulation
Chad Stone Rich Brierley Nathan is on the line troubleshooting cavanna issues we are having
Chad Stone Rich Brierley do we know when Scott will be here we can use him on TL cavanna 1
The mechanic came, and the B150 started acting up; it's a sensor that prevents the machine from starting.
Replaced worn sensor wb150 and made adjustments to phasing belt product position degree for smoother operation
The B150 sensor was replaced with a new one.

Preston Elmer

Do you have a minute to come to the truffle line?
yes
The mechanics were called to adjust the tension on the M3 belt.

Both of the useless jam sensors have been deactivated so any Cavanna Chicane faults should be true important alarms. Please tell your leads and teams that if chicane alarms happen from now on, they are true alarms, real issues and to check them and report. We had two tracking alarms that have been being reset due being used to just resetting whenever it alarms.

We found metal dust originating from the roller located between the metal detector belt and the subsequent belt; the dust is not compromising the product. Maintenance personnel state that they will resolve the issue tonight by making changes and adjustments to the roller.
Tempering chart test check update temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Weight test check update 


The weights of the pre-form and the final product look good; they fall within the limits required by the product specifications.

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications. But We still have bubbles at the bottom of the bars.

Flow wrap check update.


The flow-wrap packaging is in good condition; it looks good and is very well sealed.
Twenty trays of accumulated work were left over because the mechanics stopped Cavana 1; the reasons were unknown.
M6 belt running correctly.
Packaging seal is well. No air leaks. Eyemark is centered and the BBD/ lot is correct. Final piece weight is within the range but I’ve notified the team lead to hit target at 14g.
Pallet verification

Frederick Peairs looks like you guys will stop filling chocolate 1.5-2hrs into your shift on Monday

After speaking with the operators it seems like they don’t like placing the magnet on the correct location because the chocolate overflows. Can we follow up on this please? 
Preston Elmer Alan Greer Rich Brierley Marcos Montano
We talked to EEs about cleaning components ( nose bar, rollers, and sensors)
Yes but the chocolate doesn't flow over more than 1 and a half bars of the 5 bar magnet. It's much less effective that way. Maybe jacketing the slide or a narrower magnet would be an option.
The bags look good.
End shift. Jeffrey Barney Alan Todd Nidia Lopez Corea Sofia Carcamo

Charity Young Miriah Manzanares Curt Willis I sent an email regarding the water activity meter in L1. It will be important to take a look at it as one shot will be running today.

Hi Team,

Information for Sollich 4 Kitchen:

Today we started cooking and everything was running well. However, during the fourth batch, the operator noticed that the recipe was not mixing properly and it took a long time to transfer to the kettle, which caused a blockage in the pipeline.

The immediate corrective action was to wash the Mixing Tank and the pipeline leading to the kettle.

When we prepared the next mix, I realized that the agitator motor was not running during the addition of the initial ingredients. Adjustments are required to correct this issue, and a system reset may be necessary.
Jorge Carrillo Nathan Topham Erandy Garcilazo Ceja Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth
